svg.text: E.G.G.S. Search Pipeline — End-to-End Technical Flow
9 ingredients · 819 S Van Buren Ave, Dallas TX 75208 · 10 mile radius
The 9 ingredient items are treated as a single "Shopping List" dish.
Each item + quantity sent to Claude for normalization.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5 · Max tokens: 4096 · JSON mode: ON
System: "Professional culinary assistant — consolidate, normalize units, add prep waste"
Returns: IngredientLine[] with id (UUID), name, quantity, unit, category, sources[]
Server assigns crypto.randomUUID() to each ingredient (prevents AI UUID collisions)
Result persisted to Supabase ingredient_pool table if storeToIngredientPool=true
POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages · claude-haiku-4-5 · 2048 tokens · JSON mode
Evaluates: "Does the spec change which SKU to buy?"
Example: "Peanut butter — Creamy or Crunchy?" options: ["Creamy", "Crunchy", "No preference"]
Example: "Soy sauce — Regular or Low Sodium?" options: ["Regular", "Low Sodium", "Tamari"]
Returns [] if all 9 ingredients are precise enough. User can also click "Skip" to proceed.
POST /api/price-plan → Kroger API (real prices) then Anthropic (fallback for misses)
KROGER API CALL #1 — Find Nearby Stores
Auth: Bearer token (OAuth2 client_credentials → product.compact scope)
Returns: locationId, name, address for each Kroger/Tom Thumb in radius
KROGER API CALL #2-10 — Search Products (×9 in parallel)
Promise.allSettled() — all 9 searches run simultaneously against primaryLocation
"2% milk" → Kroger 2% Reduced Fat Milk, 1 gal · $3.49 reg / $2.99 promo
"eggs" → Kroger Grade A Large Eggs, 24 ct · $4.99 reg
"flour" → Kroger All Purpose Flour, 5 lb · $2.99 reg
"rice krispies" → Kellogg's Rice Krispies, 12 oz · $4.29 reg
"white bread" → Mrs Baird's White Bread, 20 oz · $3.49 reg
"gouda cheese" → Kroger Gouda Cheese Slices, 7 oz · $3.99 reg
"soy sauce" → Kikkoman Soy Sauce, 15 oz · $3.29 reg
"peanut butter" → Jif Creamy Peanut Butter, 16 oz · $3.79 reg
HOW PRODUCT URLs AND PROOF LINKS ARE DETERMINED
productUrl built from Kroger response data:
proofUrl = productUrl (same link — Kroger product page IS the proof)
Price data: regular + promo from Kroger's items[0].price object
Claude is instructed to use web search to find actual prices.
proofUrl = URL Claude actually visited and extracted price from.
productUrl = retailer product page (may differ from proofUrl)
CRITICAL: Claude is told "NEVER fabricate a URL"
If no real URL found → proofUrl omitted → confidence = "estimated"
ANTHROPIC API CALL #3 — AI Price Search (Web Search Fallback)
POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages · claude-haiku-4-5 · 6000 tokens · JSON mode
System: "Professional grocery price research assistant — use web search for real prices"
"Find lowest prices for: [only ingredients Kroger missed]"
Claude uses web search to visit retailer pages → returns StorePlan[] with prices + URLs
Confidence per item: "real" (confirmed from product page), "estimated_with_source" (URL found),
All 9 Kroger items → single StorePlan { storeName: "Tom Thumb #1234", priceSource: "kroger_api" }
subtotal calculated, tax = subtotal × 0.0825 (hardcoded 8.25%), grandTotal = subtotal + tax
Only present if Kroger had misses. May contain 1-3 additional stores.
Each item has its own confidence level and optional proofUrl.
allStores.slice(0, settings.maxStores) — Kroger first, then AI stores up to limit.
4. Persist to Supabase & update event status
4343 W Illinois Ave, Dallas TX · 0 mi · Physical
User must add to Kroger cart manually on kroger.com
No cart API integration — individual product links only
Opens proofUrl (where Claude found the price) in new tab
Not a purchase link — informational source verification only
TOTAL EXTERNAL API CALLS FOR THIS SEARCH
3. AI price search — only if Kroger misses (6000 tokens)
